title: podman load results in permissions denied on /etc/passwd
body:
### Issue Description

When an image is loaded using `podman load -i <image.tar>` (exported from a different system) it stops with an error instead of being successful:
`docker-archive: writing blob: adding layer with blob "sha256:6c3e7df31590f02f10cb71fc4eb27653e9b428df2e6e5421a455b062bd2e39f9": processing tar file(open /etc/passwd: permission denied): exit status 1`

The error occurs with root and in unprivileged mode. Also other commands aren't successful:
podman pull docker-archive:<image.tar>
podman import <image.tar>

The rootless_storage_path has been changed to another location within the /etc/containers/storage.conf

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Have another rootless_storage_path configured in /etc/containers/storage.conf
2. Export docker image on foreign system & copy it to the target server
3. Perform a `pull load -i <image.tar>`


### Describe the results you received

Describe the results you received

### Describe the results you expected

**podman load -i owncloud_server_10.13.3.tar**
```

 * oci-archive: loading index: open /var/tmp/oci192597698/index.json: no such file or directory
 * docker-archive: writing blob: adding layer with blob "sha256:6c3e7df31590f02f10cb71fc4eb27653e9b428df2e6e5421a455b062bd2e39f9": processing tar file(open /etc/passwd: permission denied): exit status 1

```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

The server is a virtual machine located within an air gapped environment.

### Additional information

Even if SELinux is set to permissive the following commands has been issued:
```
semanage fcontext -a -e /home/[^/]+/\.local/share/containers /data/containers/[^/]+
restorecon -R -v /data/containers/[^/]+
```
Unfortunately I'm not an expert in SELinux and don't know if it would be enough...

comments.body:
@Lakrimar are you saying this happens even if SELinux is in permissive mode?  You are running rootless containers when this happens?

Do you see any AVC messages.  `sudo ausearch -m avc -ts recent`

Yes, I'm running rootless containers. Strange is that I was able to load the images before but currently it is not working.

The status of SELinux is:
```
SELinux status:                 enabled
SELinuxfs mount:                /sys/fs/selinux
SELinux root directory:         /etc/selinux
Loaded policy name:             targeted
Current mode:                   permissive
Mode from config file:          permissive
Policy MLS status:              enabled
Policy deny_unknown status:     allowed
Memory protection checking:     actual (secure)
Max kernel policy version:      33
```

`sudo ausearch -m avc -ts recent` just returns `<no matches>`

Could you try as root, to see if this is an issue with User Namespace. 

To verify this is really not an SELinux issue. could you setenforce 0.
With root the same error occurs. I also performed setenforce 0 but the issue remains...

Ok so not SELinux and not User Namespace.

Do you have a repeater?  And example of the tar file where this happens?

Currently there is no repeater in between. My colleagues download the image and provide it as tar file to me. The affected image is ownCloud 10.13.3 ([ownCloud@Docker](https://hub.docker.com/layers/owncloud/server/10.13.3/images/sha256-185ba2121efd362f33b1f1f9cdb2a1f1a29cfeface3be5810d46bf632b330453?context=explore)). I also tried to pull it on another VM directly with podman, export it and tried a load but the error remains.
This morning I tried to load the latest ownCloud image 10.13.4 but the result is the same. On my Mint system I was able to pull and save it but on the air gapped RHEL8 it cannot be loaded.
Could you try with a VFS storage?  It could be complaining about an Overlay device file being in the image.
I can give it a try. Unfortunately I'm off for two weeks and can follow up again in January...

it would be helpful to get access to the tarball that causes the problem, or if you could strace podman load with `podman unshare strace -o log -f -s 1000 podman load -i ....`, then attach the `log` file you got so we can see where exactly it is failing.

What is the file system used by `/data/containers/lakrimar/storage`?  Is it a network file system (in this case CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE required by rootless podman is not honored in a user namespace).

I've tried with different images, including ownCloud 10.13.3,  and `podman load -i` works fine in rootless mode.
Hi @giuseppe. The filesystem is XFS on a LVM device within a virtual machine. How the storage is attached to the VM-host I don't know exactly. I'll do the strace when I'm back...

Hi @giuseppe 
please find the log in the attachments. It is a 7zip file (other formats where too big). I just renamed it to get it uploaded to GitHub
[podman.zip](https://github.com/containers/podman/files/13862792/podman.zip)

Hi @rhatdan I've tried it with the vfs driver as command line option without changing the storage.conf at all. Unfortunately the error remains.

```
[lakrimar@rhel-oc-1 owncloud-images]$ podman load --storage-driver=vfs --root $HOME/vfs-test/ -i owncloud_10.13.4.tar
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 3a03f09d2129 done

Copying blob c7a67e6bdc4f done
Copying blob 6e0fb2c85f50 done
Copying blob fd99d95ff202 done
Copying blob e9b88f175639 done
Copying blob 4ae9e9ed8f0e done
Copying blob 5f70bf18a086 done
Copying blob d0bd83b24ccf done
Copying blob 4e921b35b8bb done
Copying blob 5f70bf18a086 skipped: already exists
Copying blob 1e7ead443e7b done
Copying blob 44fe174eebb1 done
Copying blob e340c36db1b5 done
Copying blob 76afb392aaa1 done
Copying blob 5f70bf18a086 skipped: already exists
Error: payload does not match any of the supported image formats:
 * oci: initializing source oci:owncloud_10.13.4.tar:: open owncloud_10.13.4.tar/index.json: not a directory
 * oci-archive: loading index: open /var/tmp/oci3352519681/index.json: no such file or directory
 * docker-archive: writing blob: adding layer with blob "sha256:3a03f09d212915b240e9d216069aba5652ed4765c7e4b098c65e71860d47b8e1": ApplyLayer stdout:  stderr: open /etc/passwd: permission denied exit status 1
 * dir: open owncloud_10.13.4.tar/manifest.json: not a directory
```

the error is happening here:

```
25499 openat(AT_FDCWD, "/etc/passwd", O_WRONLY|O_CREAT|O_CLOEXEC, 0644 <unfinished ...>
25062 <... write resumed>)              = 32768
25055 <... write resumed>)              = 32768
25054 futex(0xc000079148, FUTEX_WAKE_PRIVATE, 1 <unfinished ...>
25499 <... openat resumed>)             = -1 EACCES (Permission denied)
```

that should not happen when the user has CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE.

Can you please share the output of `podman unshare cat /proc/self/status` and try something like:

`podman unshare sh -c 'mkdir /data/containers/test && chmod 000 /data/containers/test && touch /data/containers/file'`

does it work?

The output of /proc/self/status is 
```
[lakrimar@rhel-oc-1 owncloud-images]$ podman unshare cat /proc/self/status

Mems_allowed_list:      0

nonvoluntary_ctxt_switches:     0
```

The creation of the folder and the file worked fine:
```
[lakrimar@rhel-oc-1 owncloud-images]$ podman unshare sh -c 'mkdir /data/containers/test && chmod 000 /data/containers/test && touch /data/containers/file'
[lakrimar@rhel-oc-1 owncloud-images]$ ll /data/containers/
total 0
-rw-r--r--. 1 lakrimar users  0 Jan 10 15:50 file
drwxr-xr-x. 2 lakrimar users  6 Aug  8 11:24 owncloud-volumes
drwxr-xr-x. 3 root    root  21 Jan 10 10:37 root
d---------. 2 lakrimar users  6 Jan 10 15:50 test
drwxr-xr-x. 3 lakrimar users 21 Aug  8 11:24 lakrimar
```
I also tested it within my lakrimar folder and also there it worked without issues.
I had a similar issue. Installing docker on the same rhel8 host had similar behavior as podman. Solution was stopping McAfee mfetpd.service.
Hi @durabledata I just had a look on my system and the service is installed too. After stopping it I was also able to load the image. Thanks a lot for the hint.
